tool.name: GO Enrichment Analysis (WGCNA companion)
tool.description: Per-module GO enrichment with ortholog ID mapping for non-model organisms
tool.command:
mkdir -p go_plots &&
Rscript '$__tool_directory__/go_enrichment.R' --module_table '$module_table'
#if $module_filter.filter_mode == "sel_table": --sel_modules '$module_filter.sel_modules_table' #elif $module_filter.filter_mode == "manual": --modules '$module_filter.module_names' #else: --modules 'all' #end if
--min_genes_per_mod '$min_genes_per_mod'
#if $id_mapping_section.use_mapping == "yes": --id_mapping '$id_mapping_section.mapping_file' --mapping_from_col '$id_mapping_section.mapping_from_col' --mapping_to_col '$id_mapping_section.mapping_to_col' #end if
--orgdb '$orgdb_section.orgdb' --keytype '$orgdb_section.keytype' --ontology '$ontology'
--pvalue_cutoff '$thresholds.pvalue_cutoff' --qvalue_cutoff '$thresholds.qvalue_cutoff' --min_gs_size '$thresholds.min_gs_size' --max_gs_size '$thresholds.max_gs_size' --top_terms '$thresholds.top_terms'
--out_table_combined '$out_table_combined' --out_table_mapping '$out_table_mapping' --out_plot_dot '$out_plot_dot' --out_plot_bar '$out_plot_bar' --out_plots_dir go_plots --out_log '$out_log'

param.name: module_table
param.label: Module gene assignments (from WGCNA)
param.help: The 'Module gene assignments' output from the WGCNA tool. Must contain a gene ID column and a module color column.

param.name: sel_modules_table
param.label: Selected modules table (from WGCNA)
param.help: The 'Selected modules summary' output from the WGCNA tool. Only modules listed here will be analyzed.

param.name: module_names
param.label: Module names (comma-separated)
param.help: Example: turquoise,blue,brown. Names must match the module colors in the gene assignments table exactly.

param.name: min_genes_per_mod
param.label: Minimum genes per module for enrichment
param.help: Modules with fewer genes (after mapping) than this threshold are skipped. Modules with very few genes produce unreliable enrichment results. Default: 10

param.name: use_mapping
param.label: Transcript ID mapping (for non-model organisms)
param.help: Use this when your gene IDs are species-specific (e.g. sugarcane 'transcript_XXXXXX') and need to be converted to a model organism's IDs before GO annotation. Provide a two-column TSV mapping your IDs to the target organism's gene IDs.
param.option: No — use gene IDs from module table directly
Yes — map transcript IDs to ortholog IDs

param.name: mapping_file
param.label: ID mapping file
param.help: Tab-separated file with at least two columns: one with your transcript IDs and one with the target organism's gene IDs (e.g. Arabidopsis TAIR IDs). This file is typically generated from BLAST best-hit results or OrthoFinder output. One-to-many mappings are accepted; all target IDs will be included.

param.name: mapping_from_col
param.label: Column name — source IDs (your transcripts)
param.help: Column header in the mapping file containing your organism's transcript IDs. Example: 'transcript_id', 'sugarcane_id', 'query'.

param.name: mapping_to_col
param.label: Column name — target IDs (model organism)
param.help: Column header in the mapping file containing the model organism's gene IDs. Example: 'target_id', 'arabidopsis_tair', 'subject'. These IDs must be recognized by the selected OrgDb (e.g. AT1G01010 for TAIR).

tool.help:
**What it does**
This tool performs per-module Gene Ontology (GO) enrichment analysis using clusterProfiler. It is designed as a companion to the WGCNA Galaxy tool and takes the module gene assignment table as its primary input.
A key feature is the **transcript ID mapping** option, which allows users working with non-model organisms (such as sugarcane, with IDs like ``transcript_002741``) to convert their transcript IDs to ortholog IDs from a model organism before GO annotation. This is necessary because GO databases are annotated for model species, not for complex polyploid crops.
-----
**Transcript ID mapping — how it works**
Sugarcane does not have a dedicated GO database. The standard approach is to:
1. Run a BLAST search (e.g. blastp or blastx) of your sugarcane transcripts against the Arabidopsis thaliana proteome. 2. Take the best hit for each transcript to produce a two-column table::
transcript_id target_id transcript_002741 AT1G01010 transcript_018843 AT3G54440 transcript_031021 AT5G20790
3. Provide this table as the **ID mapping file** in this tool. 4. Select **Arabidopsis thaliana** as the OrgDb and **TAIR** as the key type.
The tool will join your module gene lists with the mapping table, replace sugarcane transcript IDs with Arabidopsis TAIR IDs, and run enrichGO using the Arabidopsis annotation database. A mapping report is generated showing which transcripts were successfully mapped and their module assignments.
**One-to-many mappings are supported.** If a single sugarcane transcript maps to multiple Arabidopsis genes (e.g. from OrthoFinder orthogroups), all target IDs will be included in the enrichment.
-----
**Input formats**
Module gene assignments table (from WGCNA)::
Gene Module transcript_001 turquoise transcript_002 blue transcript_003 turquoise
ID mapping file (two-column TSV, header required)::
transcript_id target_id transcript_001 AT1G01010 transcript_002 AT3G54440
-----
**Module selection options**
- **All modules** — analyzes every module except grey (unassigned genes). - **Selected-modules table** — provide the ``Selected modules summary`` output from the WGCNA tool to restrict analysis to modules that passed the correlation threshold in your WGCNA run. - **Manual** — type module color names directly (e.g. ``turquoise,blue,brown``).
-----
**Outputs**
- ``GO enrichment results`` — combined TSV with all significant terms across all modules and ontologies (columns: Module, Ontology, ID, Description, GeneRatio, pvalue, p.adjust, Count, geneID). - ``ID mapping report`` — which transcripts were mapped and to which target IDs. - ``Dotplot — all modules`` — overview dotplot faceted by module and ontology. - ``Barplot — all modules`` — overview barplot faceted by module and ontology. - ``Per-module plots`` — individual dotplot and barplot for each module (collection). - ``Analysis log`` — step-by-step log with mapping statistics and term counts.
-----
**Generating the mapping file from BLAST results**
If you have BLAST tabular output (``-outfmt 6``), you can extract the two-column mapping with::
awk '{print $1 "\t" $2}' blast_results.txt | sort -k1,1 -k11,11g | \ awk '!seen[$1]++' > id_mapping.tsv
Then add a header line manually::
echo -e "transcript_id\ttarget_id" | cat - id_mapping.tsv > id_mapping_with_header.tsv
